mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which underpins corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically low unemployment rates often precede periods of market stability or growth.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE is below its 10-year average, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance designed to cool the economy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High interest rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with consistent payroll employment gains signifies strong consumer demand and purchasing power, directly translating into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both RSAFS and PAYEMS show positive 3-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon, particularly during recessionary periods.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 35% of periods where INDPRO shows a 3-month average contraction, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month increase in housing starts signals a boost in construction activity and related sectors, reflecting improved consumer and business confidence, which provides a short-term positive impulse to S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following a >10% MoM surge in HOUST, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to lower real earnings and a negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as investors demand higher risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation typically leads to central bank tightening.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 5-year average, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals a severe economic contraction or stagflation, leading to substantial declines in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment combined with high rates is a strong recessionary signal.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 25% of instances where UNRATE rises significantly while FEDFUNDS is high, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and robust GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into improved corporate profitability and sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive year-over-year growth, this hypothesis is rejected.
